nodes.title: UI Upgrade Plan
nodes.summary: Multi-phase refactoring roadmap for the Voxlblade UI. Documents the current monolithic state of App.svelte (~4,456 lines) and DamageAnalyzer.svelte (~5,766 lines), and proposes 8 implementation phases: modal system extraction, toast notifications, header/layout modularization, CSS variable consolidation, responsive improvements, performance optimization, accessibility fixes, and build comparison.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with embedded CSS design tokens and multi-level headings.

nodes.title: Analysis Ignore Patterns
nodes.summary: Configuration file defining glob patterns for files and directories to exclude from codebase analysis. Uses .gitignore-compatible syntax with support for comments, negation, and directory indicators. Includes commented-out suggestions for excluding log files, test files, build artifacts, and IDE-specific files across multiple language ecosystems.

nodes.title: Project Scan Results
nodes.summary: Auto-generated scan manifest containing the full project import map (2,449 lines), language distribution, framework detection, file classifications, and complexity estimates for the entire voxlblade codebase. Serves as the data source for batch analysis pipeline orchestration.

nodes.title: Standalone Build Decoder
nodes.summary: Debug utility script that performs a complete build stat decode pipeline offline, independent of the Svelte frontend. Loads game data from JSON files (armors, rings, runes, blades, handles, races, guilds, enchantments, perks), applies upgrade scaling, enchantment modifiers, shrine multipliers, and infusion effects, then outputs accumulated stats and finalized perk values to the console. Mirrors the calculation logic from src/lib/engine/ for debugging and validation purposes.
nodes.languageNotes: ESM JavaScript using readFileSync for JSON data loading; uses Map/Set data structures for efficient lookups.

nodes.title: Developer Onboarding Guide
nodes.summary: Comprehensive onboarding document for new contributors. Describes the project architecture across 9 layers (root config, source root, data, library, constants, engine, modals, statistics, stores), details the damage calculation pipeline, buff/debuff system, engine architecture, and state management. Includes a guided 10-step tour, full file map with purpose descriptions, and a complexity hotspot table identifying the largest/most complex files (DamageAnalyzer.svelte at 6,652 lines, App.svelte at 3,702 lines, perks.json at 3,689 lines).
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown with tables, code paths, and structured section hierarchy.

nodes.title: Global Styles & Design Tokens
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for all visual design tokens and global CSS reset/base styles. Defines a comprehensive design system including: color palette (--bg, --surface, --ink, --accent variants, --weapon-blade, --monk-glove, etc.), spacing scale (4px base), border radius scale, shadow presets, animation durations/easings, semantic font sizes, font weights, line heights, letter spacing, z-index layers, Draconic theme variables (--draco-*), glass-morphism utilities, scrollbar styling, and component-specific utility classes for weapon art boxes, wild bolt boxes, and drag-and-drop targets. Includes a prefers-reduced-motion media query for accessibility.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure CSS with custom properties (CSS variables) organized into themed :root blocks. Uses modern CSS features like color-mix(), backdrop-filter, and container queries.

nodes.title: Root Application Component
nodes.summary: The main orchestrator component for the entire Voxlblade builder application (~3,978 lines). Manages all top-level state including race/guild/armor/weapon selections, enchantment configurations, drag-and-drop armor reordering, modal systems for item selection (race, guild, armor, ring, rune, blade, handle, glove, essence), stat filtering, tag filtering, weapon art selection, draconic abilities (Dragon Claw/Infusion/Bubble), undo/clear functionality, build save integration, and CDR calculations. Contains extensive reactive declarations ($:) for derived computation including effective stat rows, perk rows, armor sort helpers, modal search with Levenshtein distance suggestions, and enchantment slot management. Orchestrates child components: AppHeader, Toast, DamageAnalyzer, BuffList, BuildSaves, CdrStepsCalc, TagFilter, StatFilter, WeaponStatFilter, LevelBar, EmotionalTracker, DraconicAbilityStats, and multiple modal components (RaceModal, GuildModal, AccessorySelectModal, WeaponPartModal).
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte 5 (using $: reactive statements, not yet runes). Heavy use of Svelte stores ($build, $result) and event dispatching. Template contains conditional rendering blocks, each loops, and slot-based composition.

nodes.title: Base Damage Calculator
nodes.summary: Complex damage calculation and display component (~2,566 lines) that computes per-hit damage breakdowns for the weapon's attack pattern. Accepts weapon hits, typed boost entries, debuffs, crit info, armor penetration, and numerous specialty parameters (dragon state, dark magic hex, echoing incineration, bombardier, cauterize, etc.) as props. Implements the full damage mitigation pipeline: resolves damage types via perk bonuses, applies typed boost multipliers, calculates effective enemy defenses (with debuff reductions), computes armor penetration (dual-branch formula with low/high thresholds), applies debuff damage multipliers, and produces detailed ComputedHit objects with tooltip formulas. Also handles DoT tick calculations (bleed/burn/poison) with melting shred, wound true damage, life drinker healing, and siphoning rot. Features daily challenge presets, defense input controls, and variant cycling for debuff sources.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component using $: reactivity for reactive damage computations. Complex nested reactive blocks. Uses Svelte transition animations (fade, fly).

nodes.title: Buff & Debuff Display
nodes.summary: Tabbed buff/debuff/neutral display component (~759 lines) that aggregates all active buffs from equipment, perks, weapon arts, draconic infusion, auto-debuffs, and True Balance. Groups identical buffs by name, selects the strongest source, and computes effective potency after applying perk modifiers (warding debuff multiplier, Minion Absorption summon boost, Endless Despair DoT potency, draconic hex/holy potency multipliers). Renders each buff group with color-coded source badges (rune/perk/weaponArt/race/cantrip), potency values, duration, description text, and conditional highlighting for the strongest contributor. Includes Melting Slime sticky buff consolidation and Toxin Transfer duration adjustments.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component with $: reactive statements. Uses CSS class toggling and transition:fade for animations. Complex reactive chains for buff aggregation.

nodes.title: Build Save/Load Manager
nodes.summary: Build persistence and sharing component (~596 lines) providing save slots with localStorage backing, build encoding/decoding via compression (CompressionStream/DecompressionStream with deflate-raw), shareable base64url codes, import/export functionality, slot renaming, and two-click confirmation for load/delete operations. Implements a slim state serialization format with key mapping (SAVE_KEY_MAP), enchantment compression (ENCH_MAP), and input sanitization (prototype pollution prevention via DANGEROUS_KEYS check, type validation via sanitizeField). Supports both modern compressed format and legacy base64 fallback for backwards-compatible import.
nodes.languageNotes: Svelte component using onMount/onDestroy lifecycle, fly/fade transitions, and $: reactive statements. Uses Web Streams API (CompressionStream/DecompressionStream) for build encoding.

nodes.title: Cooldown Reduction Step Display
nodes.summary: Small display component (~93 lines) that renders a step-by-step cooldown reduction calculation chain. Shows each CDR step as a before → operator → multiplier = after row, with a set-bonus override display (base → set CD) and a final floor step with minimum cooldown capping (CD_MINIMUM = 1s). Styled with monospace numbers, strikethrough for old values, and green accent for final values.
nodes.languageNotes: Minimal Svelte component with scoped <style> block. Uses Svelte {@const} template syntax for computed values within each loops.
